name: Writing NSW
description: NSW's peak body for writers — formerly NSW Writers' Centre, founded 1991. Offers workshops, manuscript assessment, mentoring, events and extensive online resources for writers at all levels.

name: Writers Victoria
description: Victoria's peak body for writers, offering workshops, manuscript assessment, mentorships, grants and a rich program of events across metropolitan and regional Victoria.

name: Queensland Writers Centre
description: Queensland's peak writing body, based at the State Library of Queensland. Delivers workshops, manuscript assessments, the QWC/Hachette Manuscript Development Program and advocacy for writers state-wide.

name: Writers SA
description: Australia's first writers centre, established in 1985. Delivers workshops, professional development, public readings and mentoring programs for South Australian writers.

name: Writing WA
description: Western Australia's peak writing body, supporting WA writers through workshops, manuscript assessments, events, awards and advocacy.

name: TasWriters
description: Tasmania's peak body for writers, offering workshops, mentoring, manuscript assessments, events and advocacy for Tasmanian writers of all genres.

name: NT Writers' Centre
description: The Northern Territory's peak writing body, based at Froghollow Centre for the Arts. Offers workshops, mentoring, literacy programs and advocacy for Territory writers.

name: ACT Writers Centre
description: Canberra's peak writing body, based at Gorman House Arts Centre. Offers workshops, manuscript assessments, critique groups and an active community for ACT writers.

name: The Wheeler Centre
description: Melbourne's hub for books, writing and ideas. Hosts major public talks and events with leading Australian and international authors. Administers the Next Chapter Fellowship and Victorian Premier's Literary Awards.

name: Writing New Zealand / Ngā Kaituhi o Aotearoa
description: New Zealand's peak body for writers of all genres. Offers advocacy, resources, events and the annual conference.

name: Irish Writers Centre
description: Ireland's national resource centre for professional literary writers. Runs the Novel Fair, manuscript development programs, workshops, mentoring and advocacy. One of Europe's most active literary development organisations.

name: National Centre for Writing (UK)
description: Based in the Dragon Hall in Norwich — a UNESCO City of Literature — the National Centre for Writing runs major development programs, translation initiatives, residencies and events. Home to the Inspires mentoring program for emerging writers.

name: Spread the Word
description: London's leading literature development agency for writers. Runs the London Writers Award, Flight programme and extensive professional development specifically aimed at underrepresented writers.

name: Literature Wales / Llenyddiaeth Cymru
description: The national organisation for literature in Wales, supporting Welsh writers through bursaries, mentoring, events and residential courses at Tŷ Newydd Writing Centre.

name: Scottish Book Trust
description: Scotland's national charity for reading and writing. Offers the New Writers Awards (£2,000 bursaries), mentoring, professional development and a rich program of events supporting Scottish writers.

name: Canada Council — Writing & Publishing
description: Canada's national arts funder, providing grants, residencies and development support for Canadian writers. Administers the Governor General's Literary Awards and funds literary magazines, publishers and writing programs nationally.

name: Writers' Trust of Canada
description: Canada's largest literary charity. Administers the Atwood Gibson Prize, Journey Prize, RBC Bronwen Wallace Award and provides financial grants and fellowships directly to Canadian writers.

name: Poets & Writers
description: The largest nonprofit organisation serving creative writers in the US. Publishes Poets & Writers Magazine and maintains the most comprehensive database of literary events, grants and contests in the country.

name: PEN America
description: America's preeminent literary and human rights organisation, defending free expression and supporting writers through fellowships, grants, events and the PEN Literary Awards.

name: Australian Society of Authors (ASA)
description: The peak advocacy body for professional authors and illustrators in Australia. Provides contract advice, legal guidance, professional development and lobbies government on issues affecting creators' livelihoods.

name: Australian Poetry
description: The national peak body for poetry in Australia. Publishes Australian Poetry Journal, administers the Australian Poetry Slam and represents poets' interests nationally.

name: Romance Writers of Australia
description: Australia's peak body for romance writers, offering workshops, critique groups, the annual conference and the Ruby Award for unpublished romance manuscripts.

name: Crime Writers' Association of Australia
description: The peak body for Australian crime and mystery writers, running the Ned Kelly Awards — Australia's most prestigious crime writing prizes — and providing community and advocacy for crime writers.

name: Writing New Zealand / PEN NZ
description: New Zealand's peak body for professional writers. Provides advocacy, professional development, legal and contract support and connects writers across the country.

name: Poetry Ireland / Éigse Éireann
description: The national organisation for poetry in Ireland, offering development programs, the Poetry Ireland Introductions series for emerging poets, professional bursaries and the online Poetry Ireland Review.

name: Crime Writers' Association (UK)
description: The UK's professional organisation for crime writers, running the CWA Dagger Awards. Membership offers networking, access to events and the monthly Red Herring newsletter.

name: The Society of Authors (UK)
description: The UK's trade union for professional writers, illustrators and literary translators. Provides contract vetting, legal advice, the Authors' Foundation grants (up to £5,000) and campaigning on authors' rights.

name: The Poetry Society (UK)
description: The UK's national membership organisation for poets, publishing The Poetry Review and administering the National Poetry Competition. Offers membership events and advocacy for poetry in public life.

name: Romantic Novelists' Association (UK)
description: The UK's professional body for romantic novelists, offering the RNA New Writers' Scheme — manuscript critique for unpublished writers — plus the RNA Awards, events and networking.

name: Horror Writers Association (HWA)
description: The international professional organisation for horror writers, administering the Bram Stoker Awards. Offers the HWA Mentorship Program, scholarships and an active global community.

name: Mystery Writers of America (MWA)
description: The premier organisation for mystery and crime writers in America, administering the Edgar Awards. Offers professional development, advocacy and networking for members.

name: Romance Writers of America (RWA)
description: The preeminent professional organisation for romance fiction writers in the US. Offers the RITA Award, Golden Heart Award for unpublished writers, professional development and regional chapters.

name: Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers Association (SFWA)
description: The professional organisation for speculative fiction writers, administering the Nebula Awards. Offers emergency funds, contract support, legal resources and the annual Nebula Conference.

name: Writers' Union of Canada
description: Canada's national professional organisation for published authors. Provides contract support, grievance procedures, the annual conference and the Writing for Children Competition.

name: League of Canadian Poets
description: Canada's national organisation dedicated to supporting the professional work of poets. Publishes Poetry Pause (daily poem), administers poetry awards and provides professional development.

name: University of Melbourne — Master of Creative Writing
description: One of Australia's most prestigious postgraduate creative writing programs. Strong tradition of producing published novelists and poets. Workshops with leading Australian writers and links to the Melbourne literary community.

name: University of Technology Sydney — Master of Creative Writing
description: UTS is home to one of Australia's strongest creative writing schools, with an impressive track record of graduates publishing with major houses. Strong focus on fiction, non-fiction and screenwriting.

name: RMIT University — Master of Creative Writing
description: One of Australia's most prominent creative writing programs, with a strong emphasis on contemporary forms and professional publication. Hosts the renowned non/fictionLab research group.

name: University of Queensland — Creative Writing
description: UQ's creative writing program is nationally recognised. Alumni include many of Australia's most successful novelists. Strong mentoring culture and close links to the Queensland literary community.

name: Faber Writing Academy (UK)
description: Run by Faber & Faber, one of Britain's most prestigious literary publishers. Offers courses in fiction, poetry, memoir and children's writing. Tutors include published Faber authors. A highly regarded pathway into UK publishing.

name: Curtis Brown Creative (UK)
description: Run by Curtis Brown, one of the world's leading literary agencies. Offers selective 6-week and 3-month writing courses. Extraordinary track record: alumni have published over 250 books, including Booker longlisted and Women's Prize winners.

name: Arvon Foundation (UK)
description: One of the UK's most beloved writing organisations, Arvon runs week-long residential retreats across rural England and Scotland. Tutors are professional published authors. Bursaries available. Covers fiction, poetry, non-fiction, drama and children's writing.

name: University of East Anglia — MA Creative Writing
description: Arguably the most famous creative writing MA in the world, founded in 1970. Alumni include Ian McEwan, Kazuo Ishiguro, Anne Enright and many other celebrated novelists and poets. Extremely competitive.

name: Goldsmiths — MA Creative Writing
description: Known for championing experimental and formally innovative fiction. Hosts the Goldsmiths Prize. Alumni include Olivia Laing and Sunjeev Sahota. A program for writers interested in pushing the form.

name: Manchester Metropolitan University — MA Creative Writing
description: Home to the UK's largest prizes for poetry and fiction (£10,000 each), MMU's MA is one of the UK's leading programs. Strong industry connections and a successful track record of alumni publications.

name: Tŷ Newydd Writing Centre (Wales)
description: The national writing centre of Wales. Runs residential writing courses in English and Welsh across all genres, tutored by leading Welsh and UK writers. Set in a historic house on the Llŷn Peninsula.

name: Iowa Writers' Workshop (USA)
description: The world's most famous creative writing program, founded in 1936. Alumni include Flannery O'Connor, Raymond Carver and John Irving. Offers MFA in Poetry and Fiction. The model for virtually every MFA program globally. Extremely competitive.

name: Columbia University — Writing Program
description: One of America's most prestigious MFA programs, based in New York City. Strong focus on literary fiction, non-fiction and poetry. Alumni include Edwidge Danticat and Richard Price.

name: NYU Creative Writing Program
description: NYU's MFA is one of the country's most sought-after programs. Faculty include Colm Tóibín, Jonathan Safran Foer and Sharon Olds. Excellent industry connections in the heart of the US publishing world.

name: Tin House Workshops (USA)
description: Run by the highly regarded Tin House literary magazine and publisher. Annual summer and winter workshops bring emerging writers together with acclaimed authors and editors. Known for an intimate, intensive atmosphere. Scholarships available.

name: Bread Loaf Writers' Conference (USA)
description: America's oldest and most prestigious writers' conference, held annually since 1926. A 10-day residential program with workshops, lectures and manuscript consultations. Fellowships and scholarships available. Alumni include Carson McCullers and Toni Morrison.

name: Banff Centre — Writing Programs (Canada)
description: Canada's most celebrated arts centre. Residential writing programs in the Rocky Mountains covering fiction, poetry, non-fiction, screenwriting and Indigenous storytelling. Bursaries available. Many of Canada's leading writers have attended.

name: Humber School for Writers (Canada)
description: A prestigious mentorship-based correspondence program pairing emerging writers with professional authors for individualised one-on-one development. Mentors have included Roddy Doyle, Martin Amis and Isabel Allende.

name: Victoria University of Wellington — MA Creative Writing
description: New Zealand's premier creative writing MA. Workshops run by leading NZ writers. Graduates have won the Ockham NZ Book Awards and the Sargeson Prize.

name: University of Auckland — MA Creative Writing
description: A well-regarded creative writing MA with strong industry connections in Auckland, New Zealand's largest publishing centre. Programs in fiction, poetry and creative non-fiction.

name: Trinity College Dublin — MPhil in Creative Writing
description: Ireland's leading postgraduate writing program. Combines intensive workshop-based teaching with academic literary study. Strong industry links and a track record of published graduates.

name: University College Dublin — MA Creative Writing
description: UCD's Creative Writing MA is delivered by the School of English, Drama and Film. A workshop-intensive program with strong links to the Irish literary scene.

name: Australian Writers' Centre
description: Australia's largest provider of practical writing courses — online and in-person. Covers commercial fiction, children's writing, screenwriting, blogging and more. Runs the popular Furious Fiction monthly contest.

name: Swansea University — MA Creative Writing
description: Home to the Dylan Thomas Prize and a thriving creative writing program. Swansea's MA has an international reputation, drawing on the region's rich literary heritage and strong connections to UK publishing.
